finding: The test plan contains no test for audit round recording. §10 defines `tp audit --record` (distinct finding-count rule: status absent OR != "PASS"), `tp audit --status --check` (exit semantics, no mechanical_checks), and flag rejections (--affected-files/--findings with record/status, exit 2), and acceptance criterion "tp audit --record / --status --check mirror the review machinery using audit_clean_rounds" depends on them — yet §19.1, §19.2, and §19.3 only exercise the review-side state machinery (TestReviewState_*, TestReviewRecord_CleanAndDirty, TestReviewStatus_Check) and audit prompt generation. No test verifies audit rounds append to state.json.audit_rounds, the non-PASS counting rule, audit convergence/staleness via audit_clean_rounds, or the §10.2 flag rejections.

resolved.evidence: Round-4 revision applied to spec/0.23.0.md: security heuristic keeps §3.2 determinism (path-heuristic-only for files absent at HEAD, §5.2); mechanical checks declared workflow-derived and active under --no-state (§15.3); zero-task spec-coverage entry rendering fixed to diff-only form (§5.4); audit record/status/flag-rejection tests added to §19.2

suggestion: Add tests to §19.2/§19.3, e.g. TestAuditRecord_CountsNonPass (rows with status PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL/absent → correct findings count and clean flag), TestAuditStatus_Check (converged via audit_clean_rounds → exit 0; dirty or stale → exit 1), and rejection cases for --record/--status combined with --affected-files/--findings (exit 2).

finding: Whether registered workflow.checks execute under `tp review <spec> --no-state` is decidable two ways: §15.3 says checks run 'in default review mode and the regression perspective' (checks come from the workflow, not state, and --no-state is not a perspective, so they run), while §8.3 item 6 says --no-state 'restores pre-0.23.0 manual behavior' (pre-0.23.0 had no checks, so they do not run). §8.3 item 6 enumerates the state-derived output fields omitted under --no-state but never says whether `mechanical_checks` and the §15.5 prompt-exclusion block appear. Two testers would write contradictory tests for `tp review --no-state` with a registered check: one asserting checks execute and `mechanical_checks` is present, the other asserting neither.

resolved.evidence: Round-4 revision applied to spec/0.23.0.md: security heuristic keeps §3.2 determinism (path-heuristic-only for files absent at HEAD, §5.2); mechanical checks declared workflow-derived and active under --no-state (§15.3); zero-task spec-coverage entry rendering fixed to diff-only form (§5.4); audit record/status/flag-rejection tests added to §19.2

suggestion: Add one sentence to §8.3 item 6 or §15.3 stating explicitly whether --no-state affects mechanical checks, e.g. 'Mechanical checks are workflow-derived, not state-derived: they run and populate mechanical_checks (and the §15.5 prompt exclusion) even under --no-state' — or the opposite if intended.

finding: §5.4 fixes the spec-coverage file entry text as '(tasks: <comma-separated task ids>; diff: +N/-M)', but in the §5.2 fallback path (no task has a usable commit_sha, files come from git diff) every spec-coverage file maps to zero tasks, and the rendered annotation for a zero-task file is unspecified. One tester writes a golden test expecting '(tasks: ; diff: +N/-M)' (empty list in the fixed format), another expects the '(diff: +N/-M)'-only form §5.4 reserves for the other roles. §5.6 settles JSON (`tasks: []`) but not the prompt-body text.

resolved.evidence: Round-4 revision applied to spec/0.23.0.md: security heuristic keeps §3.2 determinism (path-heuristic-only for files absent at HEAD, §5.2); mechanical checks declared workflow-derived and active under --no-state (§15.3); zero-task spec-coverage entry rendering fixed to diff-only form (§5.4); audit record/status/flag-rejection tests added to §19.2

suggestion: State the rendering for zero-task spec-coverage entries in §5.4, e.g. 'spec-coverage entries whose task list is empty (fallback path) emit `(diff: +N/-M)` only, like the other roles'.

finding: The round-4 fallback 'files absent at HEAD (new or untracked entries of an --affected-files universe) fall back to working-tree content for this heuristic' reintroduces the working-tree nondeterminism that the round-2 fix eliminated by pinning the security content heuristic to the HEAD revision. Round 2's fixed finding was resolved by reading 'first 200 lines at the HEAD revision'; with the new fallback, editing an uncommitted/untracked file listed in --affected-files can flip its security-heuristic match and renumber file-sec-<n> ids for all subsequent files, while the §3.2 determinism sentence still promises identical ids for unchanged spec + tasks + diff + findings file.

resolved.evidence: Round-4 revision applied to spec/0.23.0.md: security heuristic keeps §3.2 determinism (path-heuristic-only for files absent at HEAD, §5.2); mechanical checks declared workflow-derived and active under --no-state (§15.3); zero-task spec-coverage entry rendering fixed to diff-only form (§5.4); audit record/status/flag-rejection tests added to §19.2

suggestion: Either scope the §3.2 determinism contract to include the working-tree content of universe files absent at HEAD, or preserve full determinism by evaluating absent-at-HEAD files with the path heuristic only instead of falling back to working-tree content.
